claimText: The Kenton R. Miller Award is constituted by the World Commission on Protected Areas (WCPA), which is one of the six technical commissions of the IUCN.
evidenceExcerpt: Constituted by: World Commission on Protected Areas (WCPA), one of six technical commissions of IUCN.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the parent organization of the award.
distractorHooks: Constituted by UNEP or UNESCO instead of IUCN
WCPA is an independent UN body rather than an IUCN commission

claimText: The Kenton R. Miller Award is presented every two years to honor individuals demonstrating innovation and leadership in conserving national parks and protected areas.
evidenceExcerpt: Honors individuals who show innovation and leadership in conserving national parks and protected areas. Presented every two years by the IUCN-WCPA.
examUse: Statement-based question on the frequency and specific target of the award.
distractorHooks: Awarded to countries or NGOs instead of individuals
Given for climate change mitigation rather than protected area conservation

claimText: The Director of Kaziranga National Park is the first Indian to receive the IUCN's Kenton R. Miller Award.
evidenceExcerpt: Kaziranga Director becomes first Indian to receive IUCN’s Kenton R. Miller Award for innovation in national parks.
examUse: Fact-based MCQ or correct-pair question regarding recent environmental award recipients.
distractorHooks: Director of Jim Corbett National Park
First Asian recipient instead of first Indian
Received the award for tiger conservation specifically rather than innovation in national parks
